mnemonics.title: FLOYD CYCLE DETECTION
mnemonics.content: SLOW AND FAST MEET = CYCLE EXISTS. THINK OF TORTOISE AND HARE RACING ON A CIRCULAR TRACK.

mnemonics.title: NTH FROM END
mnemonics.content: FIRST POINTER RUNS AHEAD BY N, THEN BOTH WALK TOGETHER. WHEN FIRST FINISHES, SECOND IS AT NTH NODE.

mnemonics.title: REVERSE LINKED LIST
mnemonics.content: PREV BECOMES CURRENT, CURRENT BECOMES NEXT, NEXT MOVES FORWARD. AT END, HEAD = PREV.

mnemonics.title: PALINDROME CHECK
mnemonics.content: FIND MID, REVERSE SECOND HALF, COMPARE. RESTORE IF NEEDED. LIKE CHECKING WORD FROM BOTH ENDS.

acronyms.expansion: Moves one node at a time - used for traversal and finding middle
acronyms.usage: In Floyd's algorithm, slow pointer detects cycle by meeting fast pointer

acronyms.expansion: Moves two nodes at a time - used for cycle detection and middle finding
acronyms.usage: In middle finding, fast reaches null while slow is at middle

acronyms.expansion: Temporary placeholder node at start of list for easier manipulation
acronyms.usage: In merge operation, dummy eliminates special head insertion handling

visualTips.visualization: IMAGINE TWO RUNNERS ON A CIRCULAR TRACK. ONE RUNS SLOW, ONE RUNS FAST. EVENTUALLY FAST LAPPS SLOW AND THEY MEET.
TWO PEOPLE WALKING. FIRST WALKS N STEPS, THEN BOTH WALK TOGETHER. WHEN FIRST REACHES END, SECOND IS N STEPS BEHIND.
THINK OF TURNING A NECKLACE AROUND. EACH BEAD POINTS TO PREVIOUS ONE INSTEAD OF NEXT.
SLOW PERSON COVERS HALF THE DISTANCE OF FAST PERSON. WHEN FAST FINISHES, SLOW IS AT MIDPOINT.

analogies.analogy: LIKE UNDOING A CHAIN OF PAPER CLIPS. YOU DISCONNECT EACH LINK AND RECONNECT IT IN REVERSE DIRECTION.
TWO PEOPLE WALKING IN A ROUNDABOUT. ONE WALKS FAST, ONE SLOW. IF THERE IS A LOOP, THEY WILL EVENTUALLY MEET.
LIKE MERGING TWO SHUFFLED DECKS OF CARDS INTO ONE ORDERED DECK BY ALWAYS PICKING THE SMALLER TOP CARD.
LIKE CHECKING IF A WORD READS SAME FORWARD AND BACKWARD BY COMPARING ENDS MOVING INWARD.
